experiment: H7 Fas 1 — TPM-sealed embodiment, cross-die transplant matrix
mechanism: AES-256-GCM encrypt adapter; AES key sealed under TPM owner-hierarchy primary (per-die seed). tpm2_load verifies the sealed object's integrity HMAC against the locally-derived primary -> fails on a foreign die.
liveness: fresh os.urandom nonce signed by tpm2_quote each run (non-replayable)
interpretation: Copying weights + encrypted adapter + sealed blob to the wrong die is REFUSED at the TPM load step. This is cryptographic uncopyability (a hard root), distinct from and on top of the behavioural substrate-dependence (per-core Vcore fingerprint + FiLM).
tpm: discrete Nuvoton NPCT75x TPM 2.0 on each AMD Strix Halo box
